zh.lifeSummary: 當Anne de Bacon於大約1359年出生在英國諾福爾可巴根斯索普時，她的父親Roger Bacon III50歲，母親Felicia Kirton42歲。她於1381年與William BRANDON I在英國英格蘭肯特諾斯 可雷結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1439年在英國格盧斯特斯爾享年81歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bacon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: from the Norman French personal name Bacun, derived from the ancient Germanic name Bac(c)o, Bahho, based on the element bag ‘(to) fight, (to) dispute’. The name was relatively common among the Normans in the form Bacus, of which the oblique case was Bacon.

English and French: from Middle English, Old French bacun, bacon ‘bacon’ (a word of ancient Germanic origin, akin to Back 3), probably a metonymic occupational name for a preparer and seller of cured pork.

History: Gilles Bacon from Normandy, France, is documented in Quebec City, QC, in 1647. — Michael Bacon from England arrived in Dedham, MA, in 1640. Nathanial Bacon, from Stratton, Cornwall, arrived in Barnstaple, MA, in 1639. Another Nathaniel Bacon (1647–76), from Friston Hall, Suffolk, emigrated to VA and settled at Curl's Neck on the James river.

variants.name: Bagg
meanings: from Middle English bagge ‘bag, pack, bundle’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a maker of bags and sacks of various kinds, including wallets and purses. It might also have been used for a beggar, or for one who carried their belongings in a bag.
from the ancient Germanic personal name Bago (Bac(c)o, Bahho), from the root bag- ‘to fight’ (see Bacon 2).

variants.name: Back
meanings: English (Devon, Kent, Sussex, and Norfolk): from the Middle English personal name Bakke (Old English Bacca). It is of uncertain origin, but may have been a byname in the same sense as 3.
English: nickname from Middle English bakke ‘bat’, of uncertain application, perhaps a nickname for a person with poor eyesight, from the expression ‘blind as a bat’.

English: from Middle English bakke ‘back’ (Old English bæc), hence a nickname for someone with a hunched back or some other noticeable peculiarity of the back or spine, or a topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or ridge, or at the rear of a settlement.

nameMeanings.name: Anne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Anne de Bacon was born about 1359, in Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, England, her father, Roger Bacon III, was 50 and her mother, Felicia Kirton, was 42. She married William BRANDON I in 1381, in North Cray, Kent, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1439, in Gloucestershire, England, at the age of 81.

ja.lifeSummary: Anne de Baconが約1359年にイギリスノーフォルクバコンスソルペで生まれた時，父Roger Bacon IIIは50歳，母Felicia Kirtonは42歳であった。1381年にイギリスイギリスケントノース クレイでWilliam BRANDON Iと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1439年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアで81歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Anne de Bacon(이)가 대략 1359년 영국 노르폴크 바콘스소에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Roger Bacon III(은)는 50세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Felicia Kirton(은)는 42세였다. 그녀는 1381년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 노르스 크라이에서 William BRANDON I(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1439년 영국 그루스스터실르에서 81세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ela casou-se com William BRANDON I em 1381, em North Cray, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 1439, em Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, com 81 anos.

ru.King's Lynn St John, Norfolk, England, U: кинг'с линн ст джон, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Anne de Bacon родилась около 1359 г. в местности бакунстаурп, норфолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Roger Bacon III было 50 лет, а матери, которую звали Felicia Kirton, 42. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William BRANDON I в 1381 г. в местности норт креи, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1439 г. в местности глоусестиршир, Англия в возрасте 81 год.
